takeaways: Market positive today (Nifty +1.03%). Continue SIP accumulation on watchlist names near support levels.
Sector rotation: Pharma leads (+0.6% month), Realty lags (-16.2%). Consider tilting new allocation toward sectors with strong momentum.
29 watchlist stocks show accumulate signals: JIOFIN, KEI, LT, M&M, MAFANG. Build positions in 2-3 tranches; max 6% per name.
3 watchlist stocks on wait: DIXON, GODFRYPHLP, NTPC. Avoid catching falling knives — wait for trend reversal confirmation.
Macro watch: Crude at $96.11, USD/INR at 92.42. Rising crude or weakening INR can pressure IT/pharma margins short-term but often creates accumulation opportunities in exporters.
Position sizing: Max 6% in any single name. Multi-year horizon — don't chase momentum; buy quality on weakness. Tax-efficient if held > 1 year.
learning_questions: Do you prefer SIP-style tranches (e.g., 2% each month) or lump-sum on dips?
Any sectors you want to overweight or avoid completely?
What's your tax situation — long-term (>1yr) or shorter holding period?
